name: James Chirillo
description: American jazz guitarist, banjoist, composer, arranger, and band leader
abstract: James Louis Chirillo (born May 2, 1953) is an American jazz guitarist, banjoist, composer, arranger, and band leader.

interviews.title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: I did some gigs years ago, Warren Vache, Michael Hashim on saxophone, James Chirillo the guitarist, Chip Jackson on bass and Dennis Mackrel who was here with the Dizzy band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Mat Domber
about_quotes.evidence: we have James Chirillo and Howard Alden, two guitars, no piano. Two guitars, bass and drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mat Domber interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: So eventually James Chirillo and Mark Lopeman and Mike Christiansen arranged... And then he called back and said... I’d like to try the band for ten weeks... about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Daryl Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: And I had James Chirillo on guitar and Boots Malison on bass and it was so wonderful to give those songs a dust-off
about_quotes.doc_title: Daryl Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2024
